title: Math 5250: Graduate Linear Algebra
text:
Math 5250 - Graduate Linear Algebra
Fall Semester, 2026
University of Connecticut


Math 5250 is a graduate course covering abstract linear algebra in depth. Topics include abstract vector spaces and linear transformations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, inner product spaces, matrix decompositions, normed spaces, duality, bilinear forms, and multilinear algebra.



Jeremy Teitelbaum
231 Monteith Hall
Email: jeremy.teitelbaum@uconn.edu
Math Department Home Page
Personal Home Page




Lax, Peter D. Linear Algebra and Its Applications. 2nd ed. Wiley-Interscience, 2007.
Hoffman, Kenneth, and Ray Kunze. Linear Algebra. 2nd ed. Prentice-Hall, 1971.
Horn, Roger A., and Charles R. Johnson. Matrix Analysis. 2nd ed. Cambridge University Press, 2013.
Roman, Steven. Advanced Linear Algebra. 3rd ed. Graduate Texts in Mathematics, vol. 135. Springer, 2008.
Axler, Sheldon. Linear Algebra Done Right. 4th ed. Undergraduate Texts in Mathematics. Springer, 2023.
Halmos, Paul R. Finite-Dimensional Vector Spaces. 2nd ed. Springer, 1974.
Bhatia, Rajendra. Matrix Analysis. Graduate Texts in Mathematics, vol. 169. Springer, 1997.

Lecture notes will be developed and posted here throughout the semester.



Tuesday, September 1 - Thursday, December 10, 2026. Final exam the week of December 14, 2026.
Tuesdays and Thursdays, 3:30 pm - 4:45 pm
Monteith 227



To be announced.



The instructor reserves the right to modify or adapt this syllabus, with reasonable notice, to account for disruptions due to weather or other unexpected circumstances.

title: Math 5250: Graduate Linear Algebra
text:
University Policies
Students with disabilities should work with the Center for Students with Disabilities to request academic accommodations. The CSD is located in Wilbur Cross, Room 204 and can be reached at (860) 486-2020 or at csd@uconn.edu. Detailed information regarding the process to request accommodations is available on the CSD website at www.csd.uconn.edu.
Students are bound by the university’s policies on academic misconduct. Academic misconduct is dishonest or unethical academic behavior that includes, but is not limited to, misrepresenting mastery in an academic area (e.g., cheating), failing to properly credit information, research, or ideas to their rightful originators or representing such information, research, or ideas as your own (e.g., plagiarism).
Students, faculty, and staff are bound by the university’s policy against discrimination, harassment, and related interpersonal violence.

title: Weeks 5–6 — Complex Inner Product Spaces and Hermitian Operators
text: Notes on complex (unitary) inner product spaces, the adjoint operator (conjugate transpose), and the spectral theorem for Hermitian operators.
crumbs: Complex inner product spaces and Hermitian operators

title: Weeks 5–6 — Complex Inner Product Spaces and Hermitian Operators
text: Notes on complex (unitary) inner product spaces, the adjoint operator (conjugate transpose), and the spectral theorem for Hermitian operators.
crumbs: Complex inner product spaces and Hermitian operators

title: Weeks 5–6 — Spectral Theorem for Self-Adjoint Operators (Real Case)
text: Notes on adjoint, self-adjoint, and orthogonal operators (transpose, symmetric, and orthogonal matrices); the real spectral theorem.
crumbs: Spectral theorem for self-adjoint operators (real case)

title: Weeks 5–6 — Spectral Theorem for Self-Adjoint Operators (Real Case)
text: Notes on adjoint, self-adjoint, and orthogonal operators (transpose, symmetric, and orthogonal matrices); the real spectral theorem.
crumbs: Spectral theorem for self-adjoint operators (real case)

title: Week 4 — Diagonalization
text: Notes on conditions for diagonalizability, diagonalization procedures, and applications.

title: Week 4 — Diagonalization
text: Notes on conditions for diagonalizability, diagonalization procedures, and applications.

title: Week 4 — Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
text: Notes on eigenvalues and eigenvectors of linear operators, eigenspaces, and geometric vs. algebraic multiplicity.

title: Week 4 — Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
text: Notes on eigenvalues and eigenvectors of linear operators, eigenspaces, and geometric vs. algebraic multiplicity.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Abstract Vector Spaces and Subspaces
text: Notes on abstract vector spaces over a field \mathbb{F}, subspaces, and the abstract definitions of span, linear independence, basis, and dimension.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Abstract Vector Spaces and Subspaces
text: Notes on abstract vector spaces over a field \mathbb{F}, subspaces, and the abstract definitions of span, linear independence, basis, and dimension.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Linear Transformations: General Theory
text: Notes on linear transformations between abstract vector spaces, their matrix representations with respect to chosen bases, and change-of-basis formulas.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Linear Transformations: General Theory
text: Notes on linear transformations between abstract vector spaces, their matrix representations with respect to chosen bases, and change-of-basis formulas.

title: Weeks 13–14 — Tensor Products of Vector Spaces
text: Notes on tensor products of vector spaces and linear maps, both in basis-free and matrix form.

title: Weeks 13–14 — Tensor Products of Vector Spaces
text: Notes on tensor products of vector spaces and linear maps, both in basis-free and matrix form.

title: Week 12 — Quadratic Forms and Diagonalization
text: Notes on quadratic forms, their relation to symmetric bilinear forms, equivalence of bilinear forms, and diagonalization.

title: Week 12 — Quadratic Forms and Diagonalization
text: Notes on quadratic forms, their relation to symmetric bilinear forms, equivalence of bilinear forms, and diagonalization.

title: Week 12 — Bilinear Forms and Matrix Representations
text: Notes on degenerate and nondegenerate bilinear forms, their matrix representations, and the effect of change of basis.

title: Week 12 — Bilinear Forms and Matrix Representations
text: Notes on degenerate and nondegenerate bilinear forms, their matrix representations, and the effect of change of basis.

title: Week 9 — Matrix and Operator Norms
text: Notes on matrix and operator norms, the Frobenius norm, and the operator norm formula in terms of the maximum eigenvalue of AA^\top.

title: Week 9 — Matrix and Operator Norms
text: Notes on matrix and operator norms, the Frobenius norm, and the operator norm formula in terms of the maximum eigenvalue of AA^\top.

title: Weeks 7–8 — Positive Definite Matrices
text: Notes on positive definite and positive semidefinite matrices, their characterizations, and connections to inner products and decompositions.

title: Weeks 7–8 — Positive Definite Matrices
text: Notes on positive definite and positive semidefinite matrices, their characterizations, and connections to inner products and decompositions.

title: Week 1 — Kernel, Range, Similarity, and Isomorphisms
text: Notes on kernel and range as subspaces, similarity of matrices, determining spanning sets for kernel and range, and vector space isomorphisms.
crumbs: Change of basis, kernel and range, isomorphisms

title: Week 1 — Kernel, Range, Similarity, and Isomorphisms
text: Notes on kernel and range as subspaces, similarity of matrices, determining spanning sets for kernel and range, and vector space isomorphisms.
crumbs: Change of basis, kernel and range, isomorphisms

title: Week 1 — Vector Space Structure of R^n
text:
An n-dimensional real vector is an n-tuple (x_1,\ldots, x_n) where the x_i are real numbers. If the x_i are complex instead of real, we have an n-dimensional complex vector.
We write \mathbf{R}^{n} and \mathbf{C}^{n} for the sets of n-dimensional real and complex vectors respectively.
One can visualize an element x=(x_1,\ldots,x_n) of \mathbf{R}^{n} as an “arrow” with its base at the origin and its endpoint at the point (x_1,\ldots, x_n) in n-dimensional Euclidean space.

title: Week 1 — Vector Space Structure of R^n
text:
An n-dimensional real vector is an n-tuple (x_1,\ldots, x_n) where the x_i are real numbers. If the x_i are complex instead of real, we have an n-dimensional complex vector.
We write \mathbf{R}^{n} and \mathbf{C}^{n} for the sets of n-dimensional real and complex vectors respectively.
One can visualize an element x=(x_1,\ldots,x_n) of \mathbf{R}^{n} as an “arrow” with its base at the origin and its endpoint at the point (x_1,\ldots, x_n) in n-dimensional Euclidean space.

title: Weeks 10–11 — Dual Bases, Linear Maps, and Double Duality
text: Notes on dual bases, duality of vector spaces and linear maps, the relationship between the dual linear map and the transpose matrix, and double duality.
crumbs: Dual bases, linear maps, and double duality

title: Weeks 10–11 — Dual Bases, Linear Maps, and Double Duality
text: Notes on dual bases, duality of vector spaces and linear maps, the relationship between the dual linear map and the transpose matrix, and double duality.
crumbs: Dual bases, linear maps, and double duality

title: Weeks 10–11 — Linear Functionals and the Dual Space
text: Notes on linear functionals, the dual space V^*, and its basic properties.
crumbs: Linear functionals and the dual space

title: Weeks 10–11 — Linear Functionals and the Dual Space
text: Notes on linear functionals, the dual space V^*, and its basic properties.
crumbs: Linear functionals and the dual space

title: Weeks 10–11 — Duality and Inner Products
text: Notes on the relationship between duality and inner products, including the Riesz representation theorem.

title: Weeks 10–11 — Duality and Inner Products
text: Notes on the relationship between duality and inner products, including the Riesz representation theorem.

title: Week 1 — Linear Transformations and Matrices
text: Notes on representing linear transformations by matrices, and the effect of change of basis on matrix representations.

title: Week 1 — Linear Transformations and Matrices
text: Notes on representing linear transformations by matrices, and the effect of change of basis on matrix representations.

title: Weeks 7–8 — Jordan Canonical Form
text: Notes on the Jordan canonical form of a linear operator, Jordan blocks, and generalized eigenvectors.

title: Weeks 7–8 — Jordan Canonical Form
text: Notes on the Jordan canonical form of a linear operator, Jordan blocks, and generalized eigenvectors.

title: Weeks 7–8 — QR, SVD, and Polar Decomposition
text: Notes on QR decomposition, singular value decomposition (SVD), and polar decomposition, including their relationships to orthogonality from weeks 5–6.

title: Weeks 7–8 — QR, SVD, and Polar Decomposition
text: Notes on QR decomposition, singular value decomposition (SVD), and polar decomposition, including their relationships to orthogonality from weeks 5–6.

title: Week 9 — Vector Space Norms and Equivalence of Norms
text: Notes on norms on vector spaces, examples, and the equivalence of all norms in finite-dimensional \mathbb{R}-vector spaces.
crumbs: Vector space norms and equivalence of norms

title: Week 9 — Vector Space Norms and Equivalence of Norms
text: Notes on norms on vector spaces, examples, and the equivalence of all norms in finite-dimensional \mathbb{R}-vector spaces.
crumbs: Vector space norms and equivalence of norms

title: Week 12 — Signature and Definiteness
text: Notes on positive-definiteness, Sylvester’s law of inertia, and the signature of a symmetric bilinear form.

title: Week 12 — Signature and Definiteness
text: Notes on positive-definiteness, Sylvester’s law of inertia, and the signature of a symmetric bilinear form.

title: Weeks 13–14 — Exterior Powers of Vector Spaces
text: Notes on exterior powers of vector spaces and the exterior powers of linear maps, in both basis-free and matrix form.

title: Weeks 13–14 — Exterior Powers of Vector Spaces
text: Notes on exterior powers of vector spaces and the exterior powers of linear maps, in both basis-free and matrix form.

title: Weeks 13–14 — Tensor and Exterior Algebras
text: Notes on the tensor algebra and exterior algebra of a vector space.

title: Weeks 13–14 — Tensor and Exterior Algebras
text: Notes on the tensor algebra and exterior algebra of a vector space.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Trace and Determinant
text: Notes on trace and determinant of linear transformations, including their basis-independence and key properties.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Trace and Determinant
text: Notes on trace and determinant of linear transformations, including their basis-independence and key properties.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Direct Sums and Quotient Spaces
text: Notes on direct sums and quotient spaces of abstract vector spaces.

title: Weeks 2–3 — Direct Sums and Quotient Spaces
text: Notes on direct sums and quotient spaces of abstract vector spaces.

title: Week 4 — Characteristic Polynomials and Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
text: Notes on the characteristic polynomial, its roots as eigenvalues, and the Cayley-Hamilton theorem.

title: Week 4 — Characteristic Polynomials and Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
text: Notes on the characteristic polynomial, its roots as eigenvalues, and the Cayley-Hamilton theorem.

title: Weeks 5–6 — Gram-Schmidt Process and Orthogonal Projections
text: Notes on the Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization process, orthogonal projections, and the orthogonal complement of a subspace.

title: Weeks 5–6 — Gram-Schmidt Process and Orthogonal Projections
text: Notes on the Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization process, orthogonal projections, and the orthogonal complement of a subspace.

title: Weeks 5–6 — Inner Product Spaces over R
text: Notes on real inner product spaces, orthogonality, orthogonal bases, and coordinate-free vs. coordinate descriptions.

title: Weeks 5–6 — Inner Product Spaces over R
text: Notes on real inner product spaces, orthogonality, orthogonal bases, and coordinate-free vs. coordinate descriptions.
